stories.headline: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs Raises $1.03 Billion in Europe's Largest-Ever Seed Round to Build 'World Models'
stories.key_takeaway: Yann LeCun's Paris-based AMI Labs raised $1.03B at a $3.5B valuation — Europe's largest-ever seed round — to build 'world models' as an alternative to large language models.

pull_quote.text: Generative architecture trained by self-supervised learning mimic intelligence; they don't genuinely understand the world. Reality is not tokenized: it's continuous, noisy and high-dimensional.

stories.article_excerpt: Yann LeCun spent more than a decade as the public face of Meta's AI ambitions. Now he is betting a billion dollars the industry is wrong.

stories.headline: OpenAI Buys TBPN, the Silicon Valley Talk Show, in Its First Media Acquisition
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's first-ever media acquisition, buying the $30M-revenue talk show TBPN, signals that frontier AI labs are now racing to own distribution and narrative, not just models.

pull_quote.text: While we've been critical of the industry at times, after getting to know Sam and the OpenAI team, what stood out most was their openness to feedback and commitment to getting this right.

stories.article_excerpt: The frontier AI lab that built ChatGPT has bought a talk show. OpenAI has acquired TBPN — short for the Technology Business Programming Network.

stories.headline: Rhoda AI Launches With $450 Million Series A and a Robotic 'FutureVision' Platform
stories.key_takeaway: Rhoda AI exited stealth with a $450M Series A at a ~$1.7B valuation, unveiling FutureVision, a robot foundation model built on closed-loop video-predictive control that pretrains on hundreds of millions of internet videos.

pull_quote.text: We believe the next era of robotics requires models that understand how the world moves – not just what it looks like or how it's described in language.

stories.article_excerpt: Rhoda AI, a Palo Alto robotics startup that spent 18 months building in stealth, has stepped into public view.

stories.headline: SpaceX Is Now Pulling In $2 Billion a Month Renting Data Center Capacity to AI Rivals
stories.key_takeaway: After absorbing xAI and its largely idle supercomputers, newly public SpaceX is reportedly earning ~$2.17B/month renting AI compute to rivals Anthropic and Google amid an industry-wide compute crunch.
stories.article_excerpt: Days after the largest stock-market debut in history, the most surprising line in SpaceX's financials has nothing to do with rockets.

stories.headline: Amazon Raises Record C$14 Billion in Canadian-Dollar Bonds to Fund Its AI Buildout
stories.key_takeaway: Amazon's record C$14B Canadian-dollar bond sale, which drew double the demand and broke Alphabet's month-old record, shows Big Tech is increasingly tapping global debt markets to bankroll a ~$200B AI capex boom.

pull_quote.text: We're not investing approximately $200 billion in capex in 2026 on a hunch.

stories.article_excerpt: Amazon has turned to one of the world's quieter corners of the corporate debt market to help bankroll its AI ambitions.

stories.headline: Anthropic Brings Mythos 5 to the Claude Developer Platform With a 1M-Token Context and Always-On Adaptive Thinking
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic put its Mythos-class models on the Claude Developer Platform with a 1M-token context, 128k output tokens, and always-on adaptive thinking, alongside Managed Agents updates aimed at running agents unattended.

pull_quote.text: The agent never sees the real key — the sandbox holds only a placeholder, and the actual secret is attached at the network boundary.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has pushed its newest Mythos-class model onto the Claude Developer Platform, pairing a one-million-token window with always-on thinking.

stories.headline: OpenAI Rolls Out a ChatGPT 'Library' for Enterprise and Opens 'Dreaming' Memory to Free Users
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI is deepening ChatGPT's stickiness on both ends of the market: a governed 'Library' of shared, reusable files for Enterprise workspaces, and 'dreaming'-based background memory extended to Free users for the first time.

pull_quote.text: Recent improvements reduced the compute required to serve dreaming to Free users by approximately 5x, making it possible to begin rolling out dreaming to Free users over the coming weeks.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI spent the week of June 12 telling two very different audiences the same thing: ChatGPT is going to remember you.

stories.headline: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra Puts Nvidia's RTX Spark Inside a Windows PC for Local AI
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra is the first Windows PC built on Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip, pairing a Blackwell GPU, 20-core Grace CPU and up to 128GB unified memory to run 120B-parameter LLMs locally.

pull_quote.text: Our goal is to deliver unmetered intelligence to every home and every desk with Windows. RTX Spark marks a real breakthrough towards that vision.

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft has built a Surface laptop that can run a 120-billion-parameter language model without ever touching the cloud.

stories.headline: MiniMax's MaxProof Pushes AI Past the Gold-Medal Bar on Olympiad-Level Math Proofs
stories.key_takeaway: MiniMax's MaxProof framework, built on its M3 model, generates, verifies, and refines full natural-language math proofs, scoring 35/42 on IMO 2025 and 36/42 on USAMO 2026 to clear the human gold-medal threshold on both.

pull_quote.text: In code tasks, the reward can often be grounded in execution: a program either passes unit tests or it does not. In proof tasks, the correctness object is a natural-language mathematical argument, so the reward model must itself reason about the proof.

stories.article_excerpt: For two decades, the dream of machines that could do mathematics the way mathematicians do it stayed out of reach.

stories.headline: EnergyMamba: A State-Space Model That Forecasts Energy Use — and Flags Its Own Uncertainty
stories.key_takeaway: EnergyMamba pairs a fast, grid-aware selective state-space (Mamba) forecaster with uncertainty estimation, reportedly beating 15 baselines while widening its error bars exactly when it is least sure — a capability that matters as AI data-center demand strains the grid.

pull_quote.text: A change in consumption at one node propagates to connected nodes through the power-flow equations, so conditioning each region's temporal dynamics on its neighbors' state should yield more accurate predictions than modeling each meter in isolation.

stories.article_excerpt: When the power grid guesses wrong, the consequences are not academic. A forecast that runs a few percent high wastes money and fuel.

stories.headline: DRPO Smooths Out the Jagged Math of Reinforcement Learning for Language Models
stories.key_takeaway: DRPO replaces the hard on/off clip that PPO and GRPO use to bound RL updates with a smooth, advantage-weighted regularizer, so diverging tokens are gently corrected rather than discarded — reportedly stabilizing LLM RL across scales and precisions.

pull_quote.text: Once a token crosses the trust-region boundary in a harmful direction, its gradient is discarded rather than corrected.
pull_quote.role: 'Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL'

stories.article_excerpt: When researchers train a large language model to reason, they push it through a brutal feedback loop — and it often goes unstable.

stories.headline: Teaching AI Agents What to Remember: New Memory Frameworks Take On the Long-Horizon Problem
stories.key_takeaway: A wave of 2026 research reframes memory — not reasoning — as the core bottleneck for autonomous agents, shifting from bigger context windows toward learned memory subsystems that decide what to keep, refresh, and forget.

pull_quote.text: Memory is what turns a stateless text generator into a genuinely adaptive agent.

stories.article_excerpt: Ask any researcher building autonomous AI agents what breaks first, and the answer is rarely reasoning, planning, or tool use.

stories.headline: U.S. Restricts Global Access to Anthropic's Most Powerful Models, Citing Security Risks
stories.key_takeaway: A U.S. export-control order barring foreign nationals from Anthropic's new Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models forced the company to disable them worldwide, escalating the treatment of frontier AI as a controlled national-security technology.

pull_quote.text: We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people.

stories.article_excerpt: Less than a week after Anthropic unveiled the most capable AI models it has ever built, the U.S. government forced it to pull them.

stories.headline: States Forge Ahead on AI Rules, Defying Trump's Push to Federalize Regulation
stories.key_takeaway: Six months after Trump's executive order and a DOJ AI Litigation Task Force, bipartisan states are pressing ahead with their own AI laws, betting executive-branch preemption won't hold up without an act of Congress.

pull_quote.text: Preempting states re: AI without enacting a sensible federal framework is just an amnesty for Big Tech.

stories.article_excerpt: Six months after President Trump warned states to get out of the business of regulating AI, a striking number are doing it anyway.

stories.headline: $130 Billion in Data Center Projects Blocked as Local Backlash Against AI Buildouts Grows
stories.key_takeaway: Local opposition is emerging as a hard constraint on AI scaling: 75 projects worth roughly $130B were blocked or delayed in Q1 2026 — matching all of 2025 — even as hyperscalers commit $600B-plus in capex for the year.

pull_quote.text: The quarter reflected a structural shift rather than a cyclical spike: communities have internalized an opposition playbook, and the number of active opposition groups more than doubled to 833 across 49 states.

stories.article_excerpt: In the small towns ringing America's power lines, the AI boom is meeting an immovable object: the neighbors.
